channel.title: Blog by grhkm
channel.description: Recent content on Blog by grhkm

item.title: Change Log
item.description: March 24: Publish first 4 posts (SIDH 1/2, CSIDH, Shipwrecked Lattices 1; CSIDH unfinished) March 25: Add information about \(n\)-torsion points

item.title: Isogenies 4: 🚢 Shipwrecked Lattices ⚓
item.description: In KalmarCTF 2024, there was a hard unsolved challenge called “Shipwrecked Lattices”, authored by jo_k_er (Jonathan). In the end, I spent ~16 hours on it and was the first person to solve it (post-contest). There was one hint released post-contest (but pre-solve) which pulled me out of a deep rabbit hole. It can be found below Problem Statement. At first galnce, it is just a challenge related to a mysterious group action and quaternions, but upon studying it carefully, one sees that it is closely related to isogeny theory of supersingular elliptic curves.

item.title: Isogenies 3: 🏖️ Vacation by the seaside 🌊! And an introduction to CSIDH
item.description: [WARNING] 即係唔係error I know that there are many great resources and blogs on CSIDH - in fact, I believe the original paper is the most detailed and best explained resource. However, in this blog, I would like to contribute by accompanying the abstract explanation with some Sage code, albeit a limited amount (since we are lacking non-maximal ideals). [ERROR] 唉屌又error grhkm is sleepy. grhkm wants sleep. grhkm will finish when they wake.

item.title: Isogenies 2: So... what are isogenies again? (in Sage this time)
item.description: Heya! Welcome to my second isogeny post. In this blog post, I will demonstrate claims I made in the previous post with code in Sage. Personally, I find experimenting in Sage incredibly useful, especially since there are a few isogenists actively working on improving its functionalities. The main target audience is for beginner isogenists, but on the other hand, if you are an experienced isogenist with less Sage experience, maybe this blog will help too!

item.title: Isogenies 1: What are isogenies? And a brief introduction to SIDH
item.description: Disclaimer Disclaimer [WARNING] 即係唔係error After writing the entire thing, I realised that I explained the concepts quite poorly, and isn’t technically accurate either… I don’t really care though! If you want a better introduction, see Maria’s blog. Also, blog #2 should clarify many of the concepts introduced here. [WARNING] 即係唔係error Something something separable isogenies. [WARNING] 即係唔係error After writing the entire blog I learned about Craig’s Supersingular Isogeny Key Exchange for Beginners.

item.title: Hacktm Ddlp
item.description: DDLP (Upsolve) Discussion at y011d4. Source of this problem: HackTM Finals Challenge Source import os import signal from random import SystemRandom random = SystemRandom() flag = os.getenv("FLAG", "FAKEFLAG{THIS_IS_FAKE}") if __name__ == "__main__": signal.alarm(60) try: # parameter setup p = int(input("p = ")) assert p.bit_length() >= 256 assert is_prime(p) a = int(input("a = ")) b = int(input("b = ")) E = EllipticCurve(GF(p), [a, b]) assert not E.is_singular() assert 0 < a < p assert 0 < b < p # DLP in GF(p) g = GF(p)(input("g = ")) x = random.

item.title: Finite Realm of Random (498 points, 4 solves)
item.description: Nice math problem involving field theory.

item.title: Learning Without Errors (396 pts, 6 solves, upsolve)
Algebra (831 pts, 14 solves)
item.description: Recovering a curve from a point addition group law defined on it.

item.title: Shares V1 (216 pts, 29 solves) / V2 (804 pts, 15 solves)
item.description: Recovering information within what appears to be randomness.

item.title: pppp (117 pts, 70 solves)
item.description: Finding nth root of certain upper triangular matrix.

item.title: The Hardest Path (300 pts, 9 solves)
item.description: Shortest path on a slightly obfuscated graph structure.

item.title: Unobserved Box (250 pts, 6 solves)
item.description: Straightforward blind format string exploit followed by simple reverse engineering.

item.title: About Me
item.description: Hi! I’m Gareth 🐙, also known as grhkm online. Warwick Maths BSc'24, ??? Maths MSc'25. I play CTF in my (drastically decreasing) spare time. As of July 2023, I am currently doing an internship with Russell Lai from Aalto University on lattice-based cryptography. I like isogenies, heavily inspired by yx7 / Lorenz and his CTF challenges. Also jo_k_er / Jonathan’s, challenge at KalmarCTF 2024. I am now looking for a Masters position!
